ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S SPECIAL DEMURRERS TO THE SIXTEENTH (BRAKE SHOE GRINDING MACHINE-NEGLIGENCE) AND SEVENTEENTH (BRAKE SHOE GRINDING MACHINE-PRODUCT LIABILITY) CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. A PRODUCT MANUFACTURER HAS NO DUTY TO PERSONS WHOSE INJURY RESULTS FROM DEFECTS IN THE PRODUCTS OF OTHERS. (GARMAN V. MAGIC CHEF, INC. (1981) 117 CAL.APP.3D 634; TAYLOR V. ELLIOTT TURBOMACHINERY CO., INC. 171 (2009) CAL.APP.4TH 564). TELLEZ-CORDOVA V. CAMPBELL-HAUSFELD/SCOTT FETZGER CO. (2004) 129 CAL.APP.4TH 577 IS DISTINGUISHABLE BECAUSE THE POWER TOOLS AT ISSUE IN THAT CASE HAD NO FUNCTION UNTIL THEY WERE USED WITH TOXIC-CONTAINING DISKS. (ID. AT 584 ('[U]SE WITH THE SPECIFIED WHEELS, DISCS, AND GRINDERS WAS THE INEVITABLE USE.'). ALSO, IN TELLEZ-CORDOVA, THE ALLEGATIONS WERE THAT THE WHEELS AND DISCS WERE HARMLESS WITHOUT THE POWER SUPPLIED BY RESPONDENTS' TOOLS, WHILE HERE THE ALLEGATIONS ARE THAT HENNESSY'S MACHINES WERE USED ON ASBESTOS-CONTAINING BRAKES. IT IS THE ASBESTOS CONTENT OF A PRODUCT THAT MAKES IT INHERENTLY DANGEROUS. (TAYLOR V. ELLIOTT TURBOMACHINERY CO. (2009) 171 CAL.APP.4TH 564.) DEFENDANT'S MACHINES ARE MORE ANALOGOUS TO THE STOVE IN GARMAN. AS IN THAT CASE, IT WAS A DEFECT IN THE PRODUCT OF ANOTHER WHICH PROXIMATELY CAUSED THE INJURY. THE ONLY ALLEGED DEFECT IN HENNESSY'S MACHINES IS THAT THEY CAUSED ANOTHER DEFECTIVE PRODUCT TO INJURE THE DECEDENT. THE DEFENDANT HAS NO DUTY UNDER THESE FACTS. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: LETICIA A. RALLS, CSR # 10070

Off Calendar Dec-01-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kevine E. Whelan, Esq., of GORDON & REES (619-696-6700), appearing for defendant / moving party Hennessy Industries, Inc.
Anne T. Acuna, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Shirley Adamson, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s special demurrers to the sixteenth (brake shoe grinding machine-negligence) and seventeenth (brake shoe grinding machine-product liability) causes of action are sustained without leave to amend. A product manufacturer has no duty to persons whose injury results from defects in the products of others. (Garman v. Magic Chef, Inc. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 634; Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co., Inc. 171 (2009) Cal.App.4th 564). Tellez-Cordova v. Campbell-Hausfeld/Scott Fetzger Co. (2004) 129 Cal.App.4th 577 is distinguishable because the power tools at issue in that case had no function until they were used with toxic-containing disks. (Id. at 584 ('[U]se with the specified wheels, discs, and grinders was the inevitable use.'). Also, in Tellez-Cordova, the allegations were that the wheels and discs were harmless without the power supplied by respondents' tools, while here the allegations are that Hennessy's machines were used on asbestos-containing brakes. It is the asbestos content of a product that makes it inherently dangerous. (Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 564.) Defendant's machines are more analogous to the stove in Garman. As in that case, it was a defect in the product of another which proximately caused the injury. The only alleged defect in Hennessy's machines is that they caused another defective product to injure the decedent. The defendant has no duty under these facts. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Leticia A. Ralls, CSR # 10070 = (503/TLJ)
